title: HAD-MEEG
description: HAD-MEEG is a multimodal neuroimaging dataset combining magnetoencephalography (MEG) and electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 30 participants viewing 21,600 video clips across 180 human action categories. Designed to complement the previously released HAD-fMRI dataset, this resource leverages the millisecond-level temporal resolution of M/EEG to capture rapid neural dynamics underlying action recognition and social cognition, enabling integrated spatiotemporal analysis of brain responses to visual action stimuli.
methods_description: MEG and EEG were recorded from 30 participants during passive viewing of video clips depicting human actions. The stimulus set comprised 21,600 video clips spanning 180 action categories, identical to those used in the HAD-fMRI study, allowing for direct comparison of temporal (M/EEG) and spatial (fMRI) neural responses.

funding_references.funder_name: National Natural Science Foundation of China
